back_translation: Jacob continued his journey and came to the land of the people of the east.
He looked and saw a well in the field, and behold, three flocks of sheep were lying near it, because the shepherds gave the sheep water from this well. A large stone covered the mouth of the well.
When all the flocks gathered there, the shepherds would roll the stone away from the mouth of the well, water the sheep, and then roll the stone back to its place over the mouth of the well.
Jacob asked the shepherds, "My brothers, where are you from?" They answered, "We are from Haran."
Jacob asked, "Do you know Laban son of Nahor?" They answered, "We know him."
Jacob asked, "Is he well?" They answered, "He is well, and behold, Rachel his daughter is coming with the sheep."
Jacob said, "Behold, it is still broad daylight, not yet time to gather the livestock. Water the sheep and take them back to graze."
But they replied, "We cannot, until all the flocks are gathered and the shepherds roll the stone from the mouth of the well, and we water the sheep."
While Jacob was still speaking with them, Rachel arrived with her father's flocks, for she was a shepherdess.
When Jacob saw Rachel, the daughter of Laban his uncle, and the flocks of Laban his uncle, he went up and rolled the stone from the mouth of the well and watered the flocks of Laban his uncle.
Jacob kissed Rachel and lifted up his voice and wept.
Jacob told Rachel that he was a relative of her father and that he was the son of Rebekah. Then she ran and told her father.
When Laban heard the news about his sister's son Jacob, he ran out to meet him, embraced him and kissed him, and brought him to his house, and Jacob recounted to Laban all these things.
Laban said, "You are indeed my own flesh and blood." And Jacob stayed with him for a month.
Then Laban said to Jacob, "Just because you are my relative, should you serve me for nothing? Tell me what your wages should be."
Now Laban had two daughters; the name of the older was Leah, and the name of the younger was Rachel.
Leah's eyes were weak, but Rachel was shapely and beautiful in appearance.
Jacob loved Rachel, and he said, "I will serve you seven years for Rachel your younger daughter."
Laban replied, "It is better that I give her to you than to give her to another man. Stay with me."
So Jacob served seven years for Rachel, but in his eyes they seemed only a few days because of the love he had for her.
Jacob said to Laban, "Give me my wife, for my days are complete, that I may go in to her."
So Laban gathered all the people of the place and made a feast.
But in the evening, Laban took Leah his daughter and brought her to Jacob, and he went in to her.
And Laban gave Zilpah his servant girl to Leah his daughter as a maidservant.
When morning came, behold, she was Leah! Jacob said to Laban, "What have you done to me? Did I not serve you for Rachel? Why have you deceived me?"
Laban replied, "It is not the custom in our place to give the younger before the older."
Complete the week of this one's wedding, and we will give you the other one in exchange for the work that you will serve me another seven years."
So Jacob did so. He completed the week of that wedding, and Laban gave him Rachel his daughter as wife.
Laban gave Bilhah his servant girl to Rachel his daughter as a maidservant.
Jacob went in to Rachel also, and he loved Rachel more than Leah, and he served Laban another seven years.
When the LORD saw that Leah was unloved, He opened her womb, but Rachel was barren.
Leah conceived and bore a son, and she named him Reuben, for she said, "The LORD has seen my affliction; surely now my husband will love me."
She conceived again and bore a son, and said, "Because the LORD heard that I am unloved, He has given me this son also." So she named him Simeon.
She conceived again and bore a son, and said, "Now this time my husband will be joined to me, because I have borne him three sons." Therefore he was named Levi.
She conceived again and bore a son, and said, "This time I will praise the LORD." Therefore she named him Judah. Then she ceased bearing.
